questions.q: In which city is India Gate located?
questions.explanation: India Gate stands on the eastern end of the Rajpath (now Kartavya Path) ceremonial axis in New Delhi.

questions.q: What kind of monument is India Gate?
questions.explanation: India Gate is a war memorial honouring soldiers of the British Indian Army who died in the First World War and the Third Anglo-Afghan War.

questions.q: What is the overall architectural form of India Gate?
questions.explanation: India Gate is designed as a triumphal arch, in the tradition of memorial arches such as the Arc de Triomphe.

questions.q: India Gate primarily commemorates Indian soldiers who died in which conflict?
questions.explanation: It chiefly honours the soldiers of the British Indian Army who died in the First World War (1914-1918) and the Third Anglo-Afghan War.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is India Gate?
questions.explanation: India Gate rises about 42 metres (roughly 138 feet) high.

questions.q: What is the main building material of India Gate?
questions.explanation: India Gate is built mainly of red and pale (buff) sandstone, with granite at its base.

questions.q: India Gate sits at the eastern end of which ceremonial avenue?
questions.explanation: India Gate anchors the eastern end of the ceremonial avenue formerly called Rajpath and renamed Kartavya Path in 2022.

questions.q: What single nation's name is inscribed prominently across the top of the arch?
questions.explanation: The word 'INDIA', flanked by the dates of the First World War, is inscribed across the arch above the names of the fallen soldiers.

questions.q: India Gate is a centrepiece of which planned imperial-era district of the capital?
questions.explanation: India Gate is a centrepiece of Lutyens' Delhi, the imperial capital district planned in the early 20th century.

questions.q: A war flame called the Amar Jawan Jyoti once burned beneath India Gate. What did it honour?
options: Fallen soldiers, as a tomb of the unknown soldier
questions.explanation: The Amar Jawan Jyoti, an eternal flame installed in 1972, served as India's tomb of the unknown soldier honouring the war dead.

questions.q: Who designed India Gate?
questions.explanation: India Gate was designed by Sir Edwin Lutyens, the principal architect of New Delhi.

questions.q: In which year was the foundation stone of India Gate laid?
questions.explanation: The foundation stone of the All India War Memorial was laid in 1921 by the Duke of Connaught.

questions.q: In which year was India Gate completed and formally inaugurated?
questions.explanation: India Gate was completed and inaugurated in 1931, around the period New Delhi became the capital.

questions.q: What was the original official name of India Gate?
questions.explanation: India Gate was originally named the All India War Memorial.

questions.q: Roughly how many soldiers' names are inscribed on India Gate?
questions.explanation: The names of about 13,300 servicemen are inscribed on India Gate; although the memorial commemorates roughly 70,000-90,000 dead, only around 13,300 individual names are actually carved on it.

questions.q: Which annual national event is held on the avenue in front of India Gate?
questions.explanation: The Republic Day parade on 26 January proceeds along the avenue past India Gate each year.

questions.q: Which monument is the most direct stylistic inspiration for India Gate's arch form?
options: The Gateway of India in Mumbai
The Arc de Triomphe in Paris
questions.explanation: India Gate's triumphal-arch form is most often compared to and inspired by the Arc de Triomphe in Paris.

questions.q: Besides World War I, India Gate also commemorates soldiers who fell in which specific war?
questions.explanation: India Gate also honours soldiers who died in the Third Anglo-Afghan War of 1919.

questions.q: A canopy once standing behind India Gate originally sheltered a statue of whom?
questions.explanation: The cupola behind India Gate originally held a statue of King George V, later removed after independence.

questions.q: What kind of public setting surrounds India Gate, popular with visitors in the evenings?
options: Lawns and gardens with water channels
questions.explanation: India Gate is set within expansive lawns and gardens framed by ornamental water channels, a popular evening gathering spot.

questions.q: In 1972, what was added beneath the arch of India Gate to mark a more recent conflict?
options: A bronze relief of the Mahabharata
The Amar Jawan Jyoti eternal flame
questions.explanation: The Amar Jawan Jyoti was installed in 1972 following the 1971 Indo-Pakistan War to honour the fallen.

questions.q: Whose statue was installed in 2022 in the canopy behind India Gate after years of it standing empty?
questions.explanation: In 2022 a black granite statue of Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose was installed in the long-empty canopy behind India Gate.

questions.q: Which body, with which Lutyens worked extensively, oversaw imperial war memorials like India Gate?
options: The Royal Institute of British Architects
questions.explanation: Memorials such as India Gate were associated with the Imperial War Graves Commission, with which Lutyens worked extensively.

questions.q: Who laid the foundation stone of India Gate in 1921?
questions.explanation: The foundation stone of the All India War Memorial was laid by the Duke of Connaught in February 1921.

questions.q: In January 2022, the Amar Jawan Jyoti flame was merged with the flame at which nearby memorial?
questions.explanation: In January 2022 the Amar Jawan Jyoti was merged with the flame at the nearby National War Memorial.

questions.q: Which dedication appears on India Gate along with the names of the dead?
options: An honouring of the British Indian Army's dead
A verse from the national anthem
questions.explanation: The arch bears a dedication to the soldiers of the British Indian Army who gave their lives, with their names inscribed below.

questions.q: India Gate's design reflects which broad architectural approach Lutyens used in New Delhi?
options: Classical forms blended with Indian decorative elements
questions.explanation: Lutyens blended European classical forms with Indian motifs in New Delhi, an approach reflected in India Gate's restrained classicism.

questions.q: The shallow domed bowl atop India Gate was designed to be filled with what on ceremonial occasions?
options: Burning oil to create a flame
questions.explanation: The shallow bowl crowning the arch was intended to be filled with burning oil on anniversaries and special occasions.

questions.q: India Gate forms a grand vista terminating at the western end with which government complex?
questions.explanation: The ceremonial axis runs from India Gate westward up to Rashtrapati Bhavan, the former Viceroy's House, on Raisina Hill.

questions.q: Which pair of dates is inscribed on India Gate marking the span of the First World War period it honours?
questions.explanation: The dates 1914 and 1919 are inscribed on India Gate, bracketing the First World War and its formal close.
